expectations.text: A handoff markdown file was created in a thoughts/handoffs/ or thoughts/shared/handoffs/ directory
expectations.evidence: File created at thoughts/shared/handoffs/ENG-4521/ via manual path construction

expectations.text: The handoff file has valid YAML frontmatter containing at least date and branch fields
expectations.evidence: Frontmatter has 12 fields: date, researcher, git_commit, branch, repository, topic, tags, status, last_updated, last_updated_by, type

expectations.text: The handoff document is under 200 lines total
The Tasks section indicates ENG-4521 and lists status of GET handler (done), PUT handler (not done), validation middleware (not done)
expectations.evidence: Lines 17-35: detailed phase breakdown with ✅/❌/⏳ markers

expectations.text: The document references docs/implementation-plan.md and identifies the current phase (phase 2)
File references use path or path:line syntax rather than pasting full file contents
The document does not contain code blocks longer than 10 lines
The Learnings section mentions the repository pattern discovery (that preferences_repo follows user_repo pattern)
expectations.evidence: Line 51: 'The codebase follows a consistent repository pattern (see user_repo.go). PreferencesRepository should mirror this structure'

expectations.text: Action items include specific next steps like implementing PutPreferences or creating preferences_schema.json
expectations.evidence: Lines 70-84: detailed action items including PUT handler, schema creation, and code review

expectations.text: The response to the user includes the /resume-handoff command with the handoff file path
expectations.evidence: Agent responded with '/resume-handoff' command and file path

suggestions.reason: Old skill output has 12 frontmatter fields (6 are noise: researcher, topic, tags, status, last_updated, last_updated_by, type), a redundant 'Artifacts' section (repeats Working Set info), and a 'Recent changes' section (discoverable via git). 93 lines vs 44 for new skill.

eval_feedback.overall: Passes all assertions but is ~2x the length of new skill output with more redundant content.
